Description.Name: Pay Day
Description.Details: A [[DM.Factions[faction_MinnesotaTribe],Minnesota Tribe]] transport arrives at the Argo three days after the battle at Fröislandis VII. The DropShip docks without ceremony and begins offloading cargo under Darius's supervision. The first delivery is financial. Twenty million C-bills transferred directly to the company accounts. Darius watches the confirmation come through on his datapad and actually has to check the transaction twice to believe it. "Commander, Marcus was not exaggerating about the payment. Twenty million C-bills just hit our accounts. That is more than we have made on any six contracts combined." Yang is in the mech bay watching the second delivery. A Minnesota Tribe assault mech rolls off the transport on its own power, escorted by Tribe technicians who hand over the authentication codes and technical documentation before departing without a word. "Boss, you really need to see this. Marcus sent us an assault mech. A Tribe assault mech. This thing is immaculate." The weapons package arrives next. Four crates marked with Minnesota Tribe security seals. Three contain advanced weapon systems that make Yang actually stop and stare when he cracks them open. The fourth crate holds twelve large ammunition bins, enough to keep one of the weapons supplied through extended campaigns. Tribe military technology, the kind that does not circulate in the Inner Sphere. The final delivery is specialist equipment. Three sealed crates with components and systems that Farah immediately identifies as extremely rare, possibly prototype-grade hardware that would cost a fortune on the open market if it was even available for purchase. Darius reviews the manifest one more time. "Six million C-bills. An assault mech. Tribe weapons technology with ammunition stockpiles. Advanced equipment. Marcus called this compensation and an apology fee for the false nuclear threat. I think he actually meant it." Yang runs his hand along the assault mech's armor plating. "We earned this. We stopped a genocide weapon and survived two consecutive battles on a frozen hell planet. If anything, this barely covers what we went through." The Minnesota Tribe transport detaches from the Argo and begins its departure sequence. No further communications. No ceremonies. Just the delivery and departure. Darius closes the manifest file. "Well. Back to normal mercenary work. Except now we can actually afford to do it properly." Hours later, a final encrypted message arrives from Marcus Hammerick. No words. Just updated star charts showing the true location of Minnesota Tribe settlements deep in the Periphery. A gesture of trust. Or perhaps a warning to never look for them. The other rewards sit in the cargo bay. Proof that the nightmare on Fröislandis VII actually happened and actually ended.

Description.Name: [End Campaign]
Description.Details: This marks the end of the "Ghosts of Retribution" campaign. The Minnesota Tribe still holds some Deep Periphery systems and can expand on the map. The Ghost Bear Watch has left all the systems Arelle took control over, they have no interest to control remote systems with no actual value. A continuation of the story will come in the future, but it will require several optional modules unlike this campaign. Thanks for playing the campaign, and we hope it was a good experience.
